basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Luqmān — the sūra of the sage to whom God gave Wisdom (ḥikma) — makes a father's counsel to his son the heart of its teaching. It opens on the wise Book, guidance and mercy for the doers of good, then sets against those who “buy idle talk” the certainty of the Hereafter. Then comes the figure of Luqmān, to whom it was said: “Be thankful to God.” Qushayrī heaps up definitions of ḥikma: rightness in intelligence, resolve, and speech; following the path by the Real's enabling grace and not by the soul's ambition; not being under the sway of passion; knowing one's own measure so as not to stretch one's legs beyond one's blanket. The counsels follow one another like a manual of adab: associate nothing with God, for idolatry is a tremendous wrong; be good to your parents, yet do not obey them in disbelief; know that God brings forth even the weight of a mustard seed; establish the prayer, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient; do not turn your cheek away in scorn, do not walk the earth exultantly, be modest in your gait and lower your voice. Qushayrī makes of this restraint a science of the soul: whoever knows that his Master sees him does not exalt himself, but lowers himself and grows small. The sūra then unfolds the signs — the heavens without pillars, the blessings outward and inward, the ship upon the sea — and culminates on the inexhaustible Word: “if all the trees were pens and the sea, swelled by seven seas, were ink, the words of God would not be exhausted.” It is sealed on the five things of the Unseen that God alone knows.
themes: the father's counsel to his son

versets.ar: الم
versets.tr: alif lām mīm
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in these three letters a trace of the Attributes: the alif points to His bounties (ālāʾ), the lām to His gentleness and giving (luṭf, ʿaṭāʾ), the mīm to His glory and splendour (majd, sanāʾ). By His bounties He lifts denial from the hearts of His intimates; by His gentleness He establishes love in the secret of His elect; by His glory He has no need of any of His creation, rich in His majesty alone.

mots.def: three opening letters, read by Qushayrī as signs of the Attributes.

versets.ar: تِلْكَ آيَاتُ الْكِتَابِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: tilka āyātu l-kitābi l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: These are the verses of the wise Book:
versets.commentaire: “The verses of the wise Book”: Qushayrī notes that this Book is the one preserved from all alteration and change. The epithet “wise” (ḥakīm) marks that the Word holds its own safeguard within it, secure from corruption.

mots.racine: أ ي ي
ك ت ب / ح ك م
mots.def: ḥakīm: wise; preserved from all alteration.

versets.ar: هُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لِلْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: hudan wa-raḥmatan li-l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: a guidance and a mercy for those who do good,
themes: guidance for the doers of good
versets.commentaire: “Guidance and mercy for the doers of good”: Qushayrī glosses that this Book is guidance and clarity, mercy and proof, for the doers of good who know God and perform His worship as though they saw Him. And he sets a condition: the doer of good is good also toward God's servants, the near and the far, the obedient and the rebel.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ر ح م
ح س ن
mots.def: muḥsin: one who does good, who worships as if seeing God.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكَاةَ وَهُم بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yuqīmūna ṣ-ṣalāta wa-yuʾtūna z-zakāta wa-hum bi-l-ākhirati hum yūqinūna
versets.trad: those who establish the prayer and give the alms, and who are certain of the Hereafter.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the outer and inner conditions of prayer. Outwardly: covering nakedness, purifying, facing the qibla, knowing the time has come, standing in a pure place. Inwardly: purifying the secret of all attachment, veiling the nakedness of the inmost by cleansing it of faults — for God always sees them; if you wish God not to see your faults, guard against having any. The pure place then becomes the heart's standing at the limit it is permitted, without claim that lacks realization; and facing the qibla means binding your heart to God without fixing it in any direction or place.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
أ ت ي / ز ك و
أ خ ر / ي ق ن
mots.sens: of the Hereafter they are certain

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًى مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika ʿalā hudan min rabbihim wa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūna
versets.trad: Those follow a guidance from their Lord, and those are the ones who prosper!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who fulfil the conditions of their prayer and observe the right courtesy of their worship are the very ones who have been guided in this world and the next; they are safe and delivered. Felicity (falāḥ) is no outward acquisition but the fruit of inner adab.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ر ب ب
mots.sens: upon a guidance from their Lord

mots.racine: ف ل ح
mots.def: mufliḥ: one who succeeds, prospers, is saved.

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَشْتَرِي لَهْوَ الْحَدِيثِ لِيُضِلَّ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَيَتَّخِذَهَا هُزُوًا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yashtarī lahwa l-ḥadīthi li-yuḍilla ʿan sabīli llāhi bi-ghayri ʿilmin wa-yattakhidhahā huzuwan ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun muhīnun
versets.trad: Among men there are those who, without any knowledge, traffic in idle talk to lead astray from God's path and to make a mockery of it. For those there is a humiliating chastisement.
versets.commentaire: “Idle talk” (lahw al-ḥadīth): Qushayrī takes it as whatever distracts from the remembrance of God and veils one's hearing of God. And again: it is the vanity of the outward that brings about the heedlessness of the inmost — plunging into the vain, busying oneself with what does not concern one.

mots.racine: ل ه و / ح د ث
mots.def: lahw: vain diversion; what distracts from remembrance.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: to lead astray from God's path

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ آيَاتُنَا وَلَّىٰ مُسْتَكْبِرًا كَأَن لَّمْ يَسْمَعْهَا كَأَنَّ فِي أُذُنَيْهِ وَقْرًا ۖ فَبَشِّرْهُ بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-idhā tutlā ʿalayhi āyātunā wallā mustakbiran ka-an lam yasmaʿhā ka-anna fī udhunayhi waqran fa-bashshirhu bi-ʿadhābin alīmin
versets.trad: When Our verses are recited to such a one, he turns away in arrogance as though he had not heard them, as though there were a deafness in his ears. Give him tidings of a painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he whose aspiration is divided and whose heart is scattered — the multiplying of exhortations only increases his aversion and recoil; his hearing is as no hearing, and the admonition addressed to him is scattered dust. He cites the verse: “When I reproach the listless, I only trace with my pen letters upon water.”

mots.racine: و ل ي / ك ب ر
أ ذ ن / و ق ر
mots.sens: as though his ears were stopped

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ النَّعِيمِ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti lahum jannātu n-naʿīmi
versets.trad: Truly, for those who believe and do righteous deeds there are the Gardens of delight.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the verse: “they believed” means they attested the truth; “they did righteous deeds” means they realized it. Their realization refers back to their attestation: they were delivered and saved, abiding forever in their rest, never to depart.

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.def: āmana: to attest the truth, to believe.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ن ع م

versets.ar: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقًّا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhā waʿda llāhi ḥaqqan wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Therein they shall abide forever. The promise of God is true, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: “The promise of God is true”: Qushayrī ties this permanence to the believers' deliverance — they abide in their rest, enduring, never leaving it. The verse's seal, “the Mighty, the Wise” (al-ʿazīz al-ḥakīm), guarantees that what is promised by such a Name cannot fail.

mots.racine: خ ل د
و ع د / ح ق ق
mots.sens: the promise of God, in truth

versets.ar: خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ تَرَوْنَهَا ۖ وَأَلْقَىٰ فِي الْأَرْضِ رَوَاسِيَ أَن تَمِيدَ بِكُمْ وَبَثَّ فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ دَابَّةٍ ۚ وَأَنزَلْنَا مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَنبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍ كَرِيمٍ
versets.tr: khalaqa s-samāwāti bi-ghayri ʿamadin tarawnahā wa-alqā fī l-arḍi rawāsiya an tamīda bikum wa-baththa fīhā min kulli dābbatin wa-anzalnā mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-anbatnā fīhā min kulli zawjin karīmin
versets.trad: He created the heavens without pillars that you can see; He cast upon the earth firm mountains lest it sway with you, and He scattered upon it every kind of creature. And We sent down water from the sky, and made grow therein every noble kind.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He held the heavens by His power, without pillar, without support or stay, by God's decree alone, His measure, His will and His governance. He also reads the mountains on two planes: outwardly the peaks, but in reality the substitutes and pillars (abdāl, awtād) who are the succour of creation — through them He protects them and turns calamity from the near and the far. And the water from the sky descends from the outer sky upon meadows of greenery, and from the inner sky upon the meadows of those drawn near and present.

mots.racine: ع م د / ر أ ي
ر س و
ز و ج / ك ر م
mots.def: zawj: kind, pair; karīm: noble, fruitful.

versets.ar: هَٰذَا خَلْقُ اللَّهِ فَأَرُونِي مَاذَا خَلَقَ الَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِ ۚ بَلِ الظَّالِمُونَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: hādhā khalqu llāhi fa-arūnī mādhā khalaqa lladhīna min dūnihi bali ẓ-ẓālimūna fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: This is the creation of God! Now show me what those besides Him have created! Truly, the wrongdoers are in manifest error.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī restates the challenge in terms of majesty: this is the creation of God, Mighty in His grandeur; show me then what those you have worshipped besides Him have created, in His earth and His sky. The idols' inability to create unmasks the emptiness of their worship.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
mots.sens: this is the creation of God

mots.racine: ر أ ي
mots.def: arā: to make see, to show.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا لُقْمَانَ الْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ حَمِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā luqmāna l-ḥikmata ani shkur lillāhi wa-man yashkur fa-innamā yashkuru li-nafsihi wa-man kafara fa-inna llāha ghaniyyun ḥamīdun
versets.trad: Indeed We gave wisdom to Luqmān: “Be thankful to God. Whoever is thankful, his thankfulness profits only himself; and whoever is ungrateful — truly God is Self-sufficient, Praiseworthy.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the definitions of wisdom (ḥikma): rightness in intelligence, in resolve, and in speech; following the path by the Real's enabling grace, not by the soul's ambition; not being under the sway of passion; knowing your own measure, so as not to stretch your legs beyond your blanket. As for gratitude (shukr), he gives its heart: the opening of the heart's eye through contemplation of the Lord's kindnesses; and again: realizing oneself in the very incapacity to thank Him, or knocking at the door of increase, for “if you are grateful, I shall give you more.”

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ح ك م
mots.def: ḥikma: wisdom, rightness in thought and act.

mots.racine: ش ك ر
غ ن ي / ح م د

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ لُقْمَانُ لِابْنِهِ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُ يَا بُنَيَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla luqmānu li-bnihi wa-huwa yaʿiẓuhu yā bunayya lā tushrik billāhi inna sh-shirka la-ẓulmun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: And when Luqmān said to his son, admonishing him: “O my son! Associate nothing with God. To give Him partners is a tremendous wrong.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two degrees of associationism: the manifest (jalī), which is the worship of idols, and the hidden (khafī), which is to suppose that anything comes about through the action of creatures. He gives the subtle definition: association is to affirm an other while contemplating the Unseen. Then this grave word: association is a wrong against the heart, sins are a wrong against the soul; the wrong of souls lies open to forgiveness, but for the wrong of hearts there is no path to forgiveness.

mots.racine: و ع ظ
ش ر ك
ظ ل م / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَهْنًا عَلَىٰ وَهْنٍ وَفِصَالُهُ فِي عَامَيْنِ أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيْكَ إِلَيَّ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-waṣṣaynā l-insāna bi-wālidayhi ḥamalathu ummuhu wahnan ʿalā wahnin wa-fiṣāluhu fī ʿāmayni ani shkur lī wa-li-wālidayka ilayya l-maṣīru
versets.trad: And We have enjoined upon man concerning his parents — his mother bore him in weakness upon weakness, and his weaning is in two years: “Be thankful to Me and to your parents. To Me is the return.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a rule from the joining of the two gratitudes: God made obligatory both thankfulness to Himself and to the parents. Now there is consensus that gratitude to parents lies in persevering in their obedience, not contenting oneself with mere spoken praise. We know then that the Real's gratitude likewise is not satisfied with words alone, unless the intellect concurs — namely the keeping of obedience and the use of the bounty in the way of obedience, not its diversion into wrongdoing. Thus: one thanks the Real by glorification, and the parents by spending and care.

mots.racine: و ص ي / و ل د
mots.sens: We enjoined man concerning his parents
mots.def: waṣṣā: to enjoin; wālidān: the two parents.

mots.racine: و ه ن
ص ي ر

versets.ar: وَإِن جَاهَدَاكَ عَلَىٰ أَن تُشْرِكَ بِي مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلَا تُطِعْهُمَا ۖ وَصَاحِبْهُمَا فِي الدُّنْيَا مَعْرُوفًا ۖ وَاتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ مَنْ أَنَابَ إِلَيَّ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَيَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in jāhadāka ʿalā an tushrika bī mā laysa laka bihi ʿilmun fa-lā tuṭiʿhumā wa-ṣāḥibhumā fī d-dunyā maʿrūfan wa-ttabiʿ sabīla man anāba ilayya thumma ilayya marjiʿukum fa-unabbiʾukum bimā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: But if they strive to make you associate with Me that of which you have no knowledge, do not obey them. Yet accompany them in this world with kindness, and follow the way of him who turns to Me. Then to Me is your return, and I shall inform you of what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī resolves the tension: if they press you to associate with God, or to engage in what is a fault in God's command — do not obey them, but keep their company with kindness, firmness within gentleness. Give them your outward in what is no sin, and reserve your secret for God alone. As for “follow the way of him who turns to Me”: it is the one who truly returns to Him, with no remnant left in the soul.

mots.racine: ج ه د
ص ح ب / ع ر ف
mots.def: maʿrūf: what is fitting, recognized as good.

mots.racine: س ب ل / ن و ب
mots.sens: the way of him who turns to Me
mots.def: anāba: to turn back in repentance.

versets.ar: يَا بُنَيَّ إِنَّهَا إِن تَكُ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةٍ مِّنْ خَرْدَلٍ فَتَكُن فِي صَخْرَةٍ أَوْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ أَوْ فِي الْأَرْضِ يَأْتِ بِهَا اللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَطِيفٌ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: yā bunayya innahā in taku mithqāla ḥabbatin min khardalin fa-takun fī ṣakhratin aw fī s-samāwāti aw fī l-arḍi yaʾti bihā llāhu inna llāha laṭīfun khabīrun
versets.trad: “O my son! Though it be but the weight of a mustard seed, hidden in a rock or in the heavens or in the earth, God will bring it forth. Truly God is Subtle, All-Aware!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the mustard seed not only as the reckoned deed but as the apportioned share: were it a mote or less, if the apportioning has preceded it, it will inevitably reach the one for whom it is destined, without any doubt. And “God is Subtle, Aware” (laṭīf khabīr): knowing the finest of matters and their hidden recesses.

mots.racine: ث ق ل / ح ب ب / خ ر د ل
mots.sens: the weight of a mustard seed
mots.def: khardal: mustard; image of the minute.

mots.racine: أ ت ي
ل ط ف / خ ب ر
mots.def: laṭīf: subtle; khabīr: knowing the hidden.

versets.ar: يَا بُنَيَّ أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَانْهَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَاصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا أَصَابَكَ ۖ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ
versets.tr: yā bunayya aqimi ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ʾmur bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-nha ʿani l-munkari wa-ṣbir ʿalā mā aṣābaka inna dhālika min ʿazmi l-umūri
versets.trad: “O my son! Establish the prayer, enjoin good and forbid evil, and bear patiently what befalls you: these are among the firm resolves.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī roots the enjoining of good in personal example: enjoining good is done by speech, but its highest form is to abstain yourself from what you forbid, and to adorn yourself with what you command of others; for one who has no authority over himself cannot carry out his command upon another. And he gives its spiritual measure: the good to be enjoined is whatever leads the servant to God; the evil to be forbidden is whatever distracts the servant from God. As for patience: whoever stands for God in truth is tested in God — his way is to endure for God, for whoever endures for God is never a loser with God.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
أ م ر / ع ر ف / ن ه ي / ن ك ر
mots.def: maʿrūf: the fitting; munkar: the reprehensible.

mots.racine: ع ز م / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍ فَخُورٍ
versets.tr: wa-lā tuṣaʿʿir khaddaka li-n-nāsi wa-lā tamshi fī l-arḍi maraḥan inna llāha lā yuḥibbu kulla mukhtālin fakhūrin
versets.trad: “And do not turn your cheek away from people in scorn, nor walk the earth exultantly: God loves no arrogant boaster.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī brings the prohibition of pride back to awareness of the divine gaze: do not be haughty toward people; regard them from your station of servanthood, knowing that you are under your Master's gaze. For whoever knows that his Master sees him neither swells nor exalts himself — rather he lowers himself and grows small.

mots.racine: ص ع ر / خ د د
mots.sens: do not turn your cheek away
mots.def: taṣʿīr: turning the face in scorn.

mots.racine: م ش ي / م ر ح
خ ي ل / ف خ ر
mots.def: mukhtāl: one who struts; fakhūr: vainglorious.

versets.ar: وَاقْصِدْ فِي مَشْيِكَ وَاغْضُضْ مِن صَوْتِكَ ۚ إِنَّ أَنكَرَ الْأَصْوَاتِ لَصَوْتُ الْحَمِيرِ
versets.tr: wa-qṣid fī mashyika wa-ghḍuḍ min ṣawtika inna ankara l-aṣwāti la-ṣawtu l-ḥamīri
versets.trad: “Be modest in your gait and lower your voice: the most hateful of voices is the braying of the ass.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī spiritualizes the modesty of step and voice: be effaced from your own witnessings, severed from your pride, withdrawn from your strength and power. On “the most hateful of voices is the ass's,” he offers a daring reading: by allusion, it is one who speaks the language of gnosis without leave from the Real — as is said, the Sufi who speaks before his hour.

mots.racine: ق ص د / م ش ي
mots.def: qaṣada: to aim at the middle course.

mots.racine: غ ض ض / ص و ت
ن ك ر / ص و ت

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَوْا أَنَّ اللَّهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَأَسْبَغَ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعَمَهُ ظَاهِرَةً وَبَاطِنَةً ۗ وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يُجَادِلُ فِي اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَلَا هُدًى وَلَا كِتَابٍ مُّنِيرٍ
versets.tr: a-lam taraw anna llāha sakhkhara lakum mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-asbagha ʿalaykum niʿamahu ẓāhiratan wa-bāṭinatan wa-mina n-nāsi man yujādilu fī llāhi bi-ghayri ʿilmin wa-lā hudan wa-lā kitābin munīrin
versets.trad: Do you not see that God has subjected to you what is in the heavens and what is on the earth, and has lavished upon you His blessings, outward and inward? Yet among men there are those who dispute about God without knowledge, without guidance, and without an illuminating Book.
versets.commentaire: On “blessings outward and inward” (ẓāhira wa-bāṭina), Qushayrī heaps up the pairs: the outward is the existence of the blessing, the inward is the contemplation of the Benefactor; the outward is worldly, the inward religious; the outward a soul without fault, the inward a heart without heedlessness; the outward the gift, the inward contentment; the outward that you reach Him, the inward that you abide with Him.

mots.racine: س خ ر
س ب غ / ن ع م
mots.sens: He lavished His blessings upon you

mots.racine: ظ ه ر / ب ط ن

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اتَّبِعُوا مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ قَالُوا بَلْ نَتَّبِعُ مَا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا ۚ أَوَلَوْ كَانَ الشَّيْطَانُ يَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَىٰ عَذَابِ السَّعِيرِ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahumu ttabiʿū mā anzala llāhu qālū bal nattabiʿu mā wajadnā ʿalayhi ābāʾanā a-wa-law kāna sh-shayṭānu yadʿūhum ilā ʿadhābi s-saʿīri
versets.trad: When it is said to them, “Follow what God has sent down,” they answer, “Nay, we follow what we found our fathers upon”... even though Satan were calling them to the chastisement of the Blaze?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts these frozen imitators with those who rise: they could not lift themselves above themselves nor above their like, and they did not find the One who transforms their states. Blind clinging to the fathers becomes here the image of the soul that refuses to be converted, even when it is Satan who leads it to the fire.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ن ز ل
و ج د / أ ب و
mots.sens: what we found our fathers upon

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / س ع ر

versets.ar: وَمَن يُسْلِمْ وَجْهَهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ ۗ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ عَاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ
versets.tr: wa-man yuslim wajhahu ilā llāhi wa-huwa muḥsinun fa-qadi stamsaka bi-l-ʿurwati l-wuthqā wa-ilā llāhi ʿāqibatu l-umūri
versets.trad: Whoever submits his face to God while doing good has grasped the firmest handhold. And to God belongs the outcome of all affairs.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two destinies: he whose soul has risen and whose intention has been purified in God has grasped the firmest handhold (al-ʿurwa al-wuthqā) and walked the straightest road, while the one who denies is left to his return to God. To submit one's face is to hand over one's whole being to Him to whom belongs the outcome of all affairs.

mots.racine: س ل م / و ج ه
mots.sens: he submits his face to God
mots.def: aslama: to surrender; wajh: face, whole being.

mots.racine: ع ر و / و ث ق
mots.def: ʿurwa: handhold, grip; wuthqā: the most secure.

mots.racine: ع ق ب / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَمَن كَفَرَ فَلَا يَحْزُنكَ كُفْرُهُ ۚ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ فَنُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: wa-man kafara fa-lā yaḥzunka kufruhu ilaynā marjiʿuhum fa-nunabbiʾuhum bimā ʿamilū inna llāha ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūri
versets.trad: And let not the disbelief of the disbeliever grieve you. To Us is their return, and We shall inform them of what they did. Truly God knows what is in the breasts.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the justice of the return: to Us their return, from Us their chastisement, upon Us their reckoning. And he notes the inconsistency of the deniers: if you question them about their Creator, they will confess Him; but the moment they go back to their error, they deny and persist. Let their denial then not grieve the heart that knows to whom judgment belongs.

mots.racine: ح ز ن / ك ف ر
mots.sens: let his denial not grieve you

mots.racine: ر ج ع
ع ل م / ص د ر
mots.sens: knowing what is in the breasts
mots.def: dhāt aṣ-ṣudūr: the secret of hearts.

versets.ar: نُمَتِّعُهُمْ قَلِيلًا ثُمَّ نَضْطَرُّهُمْ إِلَىٰ عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ
versets.tr: numattiʿuhum qalīlan thumma naḍṭarruhum ilā ʿadhābin ghalīẓin
versets.trad: We let them enjoy for a little, then We shall drive them to a harsh chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ties this verse to the previous one: the enjoyment of this world is but a fleeting reprieve granted to the deniers before the harsh chastisement. To Us their return, from Us their chastisement: the brief savour of the world only defers the reckoning that is certain.

mots.racine: م ت ع / ق ل ل
mots.sens: We let them enjoy a little

mots.racine: ض ر ر
ع ذ ب / غ ل ظ

versets.ar: وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللَّهُ ۚ قُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-in saʾaltahum man khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa la-yaqūlunna llāhu quli l-ḥamdu lillāhi bal aktharuhum lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: And if you ask them, “Who created the heavens and the earth?” they will surely say, “God!” Say: “Praise be to God!” But most of them do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī had already noted this contradiction: if you question them about their Creator, they will confess Him; but the moment they go back to their error, they deny and persist. Their mouths acknowledge God as Creator while their hearts refuse to serve Him — and so “most of them do not know,” with a knowledge that engages.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
mots.sens: who created the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ق و ل
ق و ل / ح م د

versets.ar: لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
versets.tr: lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi inna llāha huwa l-ghaniyyu l-ḥamīdu
versets.trad: To God belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth. Truly God is the Self-sufficient, the Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī affirms the absolute sovereignty: to God belongs in His own right what is in the heavens and the earth; His decree is carried out among them in all truth, and to Him is their return, inescapably. The Name “the Self-sufficient, the Praiseworthy” (al-ghaniyy al-ḥamīd) marks that He has need of nothing while deserving all praise.

mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: to God what is in heavens and earth

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح م د

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ مِن شَجَرَةٍ أَقْلَامٌ وَالْبَحْرُ يَمُدُّهُ مِن بَعْدِهِ سَبْعَةُ أَبْحُرٍ مَّا نَفِدَتْ كَلِمَاتُ اللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-law annamā fī l-arḍi min shajaratin aqlāmun wa-l-baḥru yamudduhu min baʿdihi sabʿatu abḥurin mā nafidat kalimātu llāhi inna llāha ʿazīzun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: If all the trees on earth were pens, and the sea, with seven more seas to swell it, were ink, the words of God would not be exhausted. Truly God is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the verse a resonance of intimacy: even were the trees pens and the seas ink, until the pens are broken, the seas run dry, the pages are spent and the lives of the copyists come to an end — the meanings of what We have to say to you would not be exhausted, for you are with Us for the eternity of eternities, and the eternal in attribute knows no end.

mots.racine: ش ج ر / ق ل م
م د د / ب ح ر
mots.def: madda: to extend, to supply ink.

mots.racine: ن ف د / ك ل م
mots.sens: the words of God would not run dry
mots.def: nafida: to be exhausted; kalimāt: Words.

versets.ar: مَّا خَلْقُكُمْ وَلَا بَعْثُكُمْ إِلَّا كَنَفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: mā khalqukum wa-lā baʿthukum illā ka-nafsin wāḥidatin inna llāha samīʿun baṣīrun
versets.trad: Your creation and your resurrection are but as that of a single soul. Truly God is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
themes: the ease of the divine power
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to create the few or the many is equal for Him; no toil in the many, no rest in the few. His command, when He wills a thing, is to say to it “Be!” and it is — He utters it by His word and brings it into being by His power, with no effort exerted, no strength spent, no prior design.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ب ع ث
ن ف س / و ح د
س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.def: samīʿ: who hears; baṣīr: who sees.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهَارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِي إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha yūliju l-layla fī n-nahāri wa-yūliju n-nahāra fī l-layli wa-sakhkhara sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara kullun yajrī ilā ajalin musamman wa-anna llāha bimā taʿmalūna khabīrun
versets.trad: Do you not see that God makes the night enter into the day and makes the day enter into the night, and has subjected the sun and the moon, each running to an appointed term, and that God is aware of all you do?
versets.commentaire: The verse unfolds the signs of the divine mastery over time and the heavenly bodies — night entering the day, sun and moon subjected to an appointed term. In Qushayrī's vein, this governance of the cycles bespeaks the solicitude that subjects every thing to a service on the servant's behalf, until nothing escapes the knowledge of Him who is “aware of what you do.”

mots.racine: و ل ج / ل ي ل / ن ه ر
mots.sens: He inserts the night into the day

mots.racine: ج ر ي / أ ج ل / س م و
mots.sens: each runs to an appointed term

mots.racine: ع م ل / خ ب ر

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْحَقُّ وَأَنَّ مَا يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ الْبَاطِلُ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna llāha huwa l-ḥaqqu wa-anna mā yadʿūna min dūnihi l-bāṭilu wa-anna llāha huwa l-ʿaliyyu l-kabīru
versets.trad: That is because God is the Truth, and what they call upon besides Him is falsehood, and because God is the Most High, the Most Great.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī comments on the pair True / vain (al-ḥaqq / al-bāṭil): “God is the Truth” — the Existing, the Real, the One who makes the true true; and what they invoke besides Him is the vain: arisen from nothingness, and bearing within it the possibility of nothingness. Only the necessary Being subsists; all else is loan and passage.

mots.racine: ح ق ق
mots.def: al-ḥaqq: the Truth, the real Being.

mots.racine: د ع و / ب ط ل
mots.sens: what they invoke besides Him is the vain

mots.racine: ع ل و / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the Most High, the Most Great

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ الْفُلْكَ تَجْرِي فِي الْبَحْرِ بِنِعْمَتِ اللَّهِ لِيُرِيَكُم مِّنْ آيَاتِهِ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِّكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna l-fulka tajrī fī l-baḥri bi-niʿmati llāhi li-yuriyakum min āyātihi inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-kulli ṣabbārin shakūrin
versets.trad: Do you not see that the ship sails the sea by the grace of God, that He may show you some of His signs? Surely in that are signs for every patient and thankful one!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī doubles the meaning of the ship: outwardly, their rescue in the vessel; inwardly, their rescue from the vicissitudes of the world, and their salvation in the ships of preservation upon the seas of power. As for the “patient and thankful one” (ṣabbār shakūr): a standing that calamities do not rout, a gratitude for all that befalls him from both faces of the decree — trials and gifts.

mots.racine: ف ل ك / ج ر ي / ب ح ر
ن ع م
ص ب ر / ش ك ر
mots.def: ṣabbār: enduring; shakūr: full of gratitude.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا غَشِيَهُم مَّوْجٌ كَالظُّلَلِ دَعَوُا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ فَلَمَّا نَجَّاهُمْ إِلَى الْبَرِّ فَمِنْهُم مُّقْتَصِدٌ ۚ وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِآيَاتِنَا إِلَّا كُلُّ خَتَّارٍ كَفُورٍ
versets.tr: wa-idhā ghashiyahum mawjun ka-ẓ-ẓulali daʿawu llāha mukhliṣīna lahu d-dīna fa-lammā najjāhum ilā l-barri fa-minhum muqtaṣidun wa-mā yajḥadu bi-āyātinā illā kullu khattārin kafūrin
versets.trad: When a wave like canopies overwhelms them, they call upon God, devoting their religion sincerely to Him. But when He has brought them safe to land, some of them are lukewarm. None denies Our signs but every treacherous ingrate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the soul in the storm: when the waves of the seas of the decree rage over them, they wish those seas would cast them up on the shores of safety; but when the Real in His generosity grants their wish, they return to the source of their faults. And he places upon the Real this word of forbearance: “How often you have been ignorant, and We have returned with Our clemency — My beloved ones, how much you are ignorant, and how much We forbear!”

mots.racine: غ ش و / م و ج / ظ ل ل
mots.sens: a wave like shadows overwhelms them
mots.def: mawj: wave; ẓulal: canopies of shade.

mots.racine: خ ل ص / د ي ن
خ ت ر / ك ف ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ وَاخْشَوْا يَوْمًا لَّا يَجْزِي وَالِدٌ عَن وَلَدِهِ وَلَا مَوْلُودٌ هُوَ جَازٍ عَن وَالِدِهِ شَيْئًا ۚ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِاللَّهِ الْغَرُورُ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu ttaqū rabbakum wa-khshaw yawman lā yajzī wālidun ʿan waladihi wa-lā mawlūdun huwa jāzin ʿan wālidihi shayʾan inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun fa-lā taghurrannakumu l-ḥayātu d-dunyā wa-lā yaghurrannakum billāhi l-gharūru
versets.trad: O mankind, fear your Lord! Dread a Day when no father can avail his child, nor any child avail his father in anything. Truly the promise of God is true! So let not the life of this world deceive you, and let not the great Deceiver deceive you about God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the gradation of the divine warning: at times He frightens them by His acts — “Dread a Day”; at times by His attributes — “does he not know that God sees?”; at times by His very Essence — “God warns you of Himself.” This Day when no father can ransom his son strips the soul of every support apart from God.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ر ب ب
mots.def: ittaqā: to be wary, to fear.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / و ل د
mots.def: jazā ʿan: to discharge on behalf of.

mots.racine: غ ر ر
mots.sens: let not the Deceiver delude you about God

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عِندَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ وَيُنَزِّلُ الْغَيْثَ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِي الْأَرْحَامِ ۖ وَمَا تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ مَّاذَا تَكْسِبُ غَدًا ۖ وَمَا تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ بِأَيِّ أَرْضٍ تَمُوتُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: inna llāha ʿindahu ʿilmu s-sāʿati wa-yunazzilu l-ghaytha wa-yaʿlamu mā fī l-arḥāmi wa-mā tadrī nafsun mādhā taksibu ghadan wa-mā tadrī nafsun bi-ayyi arḍin tamūtu inna llāha ʿalīmun khabīrun
versets.trad: Truly with God is the knowledge of the Hour. He sends down the saving rain, and He knows what is in the wombs. No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die. Truly God is Knowing, All-Aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī details the exclusive knowledge of the Unseen: He alone knows the Hour; He knows what is in the wombs — male and female, wretched and blessed, fair and foul; He knows when the rain descends, how many drops, upon what patch of earth. And none knows what he will earn tomorrow — good or evil, accord or discord — nor in what land he will die, whether he will attain his aim or miss it. These five things, says the tradition cited, none knows, neither angel brought near nor prophet sent.

mots.racine: ع ل م / س و ع
mots.sens: with Him the knowledge of the Hour

mots.racine: ع ل م / ر ح م
mots.sens: He knows what is in the wombs

mots.racine: أ ر ض / م و ت
mots.sens: in what land it will die
